selection_reasons.regenerate-rederive-5x3: main-agent candidate-generation primitive prompted by repeated oracle collapse
selection_reasons.repair-falsify-5x3-efficient: pooled champion across 10 fresh panels: 53/126 exact; 38.1% weakest family

source: mechanical anchors plus iteration 13 Sol director

strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: A five-answer bank paired with the validated three-reviewer falsification mechanism may retain much of the champion's accuracy at eight calls per case rather than twelve; earlier five-solver repair systems reached 0.417–0.500 pooled accuracy.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Iteration 13's nine-answer bank doubled candidate-oracle coverage from 2/12 to 4/12, while the one-review mechanism has a pooled 15-to-2 helpful-to-harmful intervention balance. Pairing them tests whether one falsifying repairer can exploit broader generation without three-review fragmentation.
strategies.name: One-Review Nine-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Replicate the unchanged call-efficient mechanism because its strongly positive pooled correction balance remains promising despite noisy exact accuracy, and it requires two fewer review calls than the retained champion whenever review activates.
strategies.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Recent five-answer banks often omitted the correct answer. Three blind regenerators use distinct domain-neutral lenses without seeing the bank, then join its votes under a deterministic base-favoring plurality. This tests candidate-coverage gain without relying on a judge.
strategies.name: Blind Diversified Regeneration
